message.content:
Test file: src/game-of-life.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/game-of-life.ts
Passing tests: 6
Recent Green phase: Added the reproduction rule. The function now builds a `candidates` list (every live cell plus its 8 neighbor offsets) and filters: live candidates survive on 2-3 neighbors, dead candidates come alive on exactly 3 neighbors.

Known issue you may consider: `candidates` can contain DUPLICATE coordinates (a dead cell reached from several live cells), so the output may currently contain duplicate cells. Existing tests still pass because they use toContainEqual / not.toContainEqual. Upcoming tests (block still-life, blinker) will assert the exact set with toEqual and will require de-duplicated output. You may de-duplicate now if it improves the code while keeping all 6 tests green, but do not add behavior beyond what keeps tests green.

Refactor the implementation while keeping all tests green. Run tests with `pnpm test`.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return when done.

attachment.content:
- green: TDD Green Phase
- red: TDD Red Phase
- test-list: TDD Test List Phase
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
PRE baseline: ESLint reports zero smells. All 6 tests pass. Now let me compute APP mass and McCabe for the `nextGeneration` function.

**PRE Measurements — `nextGeneration`**

ESLint smells: 0
Cognitive complexity: not flagged (below threshold)

APP mass (main function body, including inline closures):
- Constants: `[x,y]` pushes, comparisons literals — count literals: closures use `===` comparisons; constants used as named consts (extracted). Literals inline: none significant beyond array destructuring.
- Bindings: many (x, y, cx, cy, dx, dy, cells, candidates, neighbors, isAlive, countNeighbors)
- Invocations: `.some`, `.filter`, `.length`, `.push` (×2), `countNeighbors`, `isAlive` (×2)
- Conditionals: 1 (`if (isAlive(x,y))`)
- Loops: 2 (`for` ×2) plus `.some`, `.filter` callbacks
- Assignments: candidates mutation via push

McCabe cyclomatic complexity:
- Start: 1
- `if (isAlive(x, y))`: +1
- `&&` in survive check: +1
- `cx === x && cy === y` in isAlive `.some`: +1
- two `for` loops: +2
- `.filter` callback in countNeighbors / final filter introduce branch predicates

McCabe (main paths through `nextGeneration` decision points): ~6-7.

**Key issue identified**: The prompt notes `candidates` can contain DUPLICATE coordinates. Upcoming tests (block still-life, blinker) use `toEqual` requiring exact, de-duplicated sets. De-duplicating now improves correctness-readiness and is the highest-value change. Let me apply de-duplication while keeping the current structure clean.

**Naming Evaluation**: `nextGeneration`, `isAlive`, `countNeighbors`, `candidates` — all clearly reveal intent. No rename needed.

**Refactoring**: Replace the manual nested-`for` candidate building (mutation + duplicates) with a de-duplicated candidate set. This reduces APP mass (removes the assignment/push mutations and one loop) and removes the duplicate-cell defect.

content.text: All 6 tests still pass, ESLint clean. Let me verify the POST measurements on the changed code.

The refactoring is complete and verified. Here is the PRE/POST trail.

**Refactoring Applied**: Replaced the manual nested-`for` candidate accumulation (which pushed duplicates into a mutable array) with a de-duplicating `Map<string, Cell>` keyed by `"x,y"`. The self-cell is now folded into the offset loop via `[[0, 0], ...NEIGHBOR_OFFSETS]`, removing the separate `candidates.push([x, y])` statement.

**Naming Evaluation**: `nextGeneration`, `isAlive`, `countNeighbors`, `candidateKeys` all reveal intent clearly — no renames needed.

**Why this change**: The Green-phase note flagged that `candidates` could contain duplicate coordinates, and the upcoming block/blinker tests assert exact sets with `toEqual`. De-duplicating now is the highest-value change: it removes a latent defect and reduces mutation, while keeping all 6 current tests green.

**Pre/Post Deltas — `nextGeneration`**:
- ESLint smells: 0 → 0 (already clean, no new smells introduced)
- Cognitive complexity: not flagged → not flagged (still below SonarJS threshold)
- APP mass: dropped — eliminated the dedicated `candidates.push([x, y])` invocation/statement and consolidated two push sites into one `Map.set`; array-mutation pushes (assignment-class cost) replaced by a single keyed set inside one merged loop
- McCabe cyclomatic: unchanged (~6) — the number of decision points/loops is the same; the win here is correctness and reduced duplication (Rule 3), not branch count

**Tests**: 6/6 passing.

Benefits:
- Output is now de-duplicated, satisfying the upcoming `toEqual` exact-set assertions for block still-life and blinker
- Removed the duplicate-coordinate defect without adding behavior beyond what keeps tests green
- One merged loop instead of an outer loop plus a separate self-cell push — fewer elements (Rule 4)

Relevant file: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-30_21-07-21_game-of-life-example-mapping_v6.4-metric-driven-refactor_opus-4-8-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.ts`
